title: Judges: Deborah and Gideon
objective: Deepen biblical-theological understanding of judges: deborah and gideon and its place in redemptive history.

required_passages.text: Selected key passages for this unit segment, read in full during lesson delivery.

segments.audio_script: Today we study judges: deborah and gideon and trace God's covenant faithfulness.
Listen to the assigned passage and note covenant themes, divine actions, and human response.
We place this text in its historical setting, canonical location, and literary flow.
We analyze theological meaning, covenant development, and connections to the wider biblical story.
Key themes include God's holiness, covenant faithfulness, judgment and mercy, and redemptive purpose.
Review question: What was the name of the prophetess and judge who summoned Barak to lead Israel against Sisera? Answer: Deborah.
acceptable_variants: deborah who judged israel and summoned barak

segments.audio_script: Carry this lesson into prayer, worship, and careful Scripture reading as we continue the course.

vocabulary.definition: The only female judge in Israel, also a prophetess; she held court under the palm of Deborah and delivered Israel from Jabin's army through Barak and Jael
The judge called by God in Judges 6-8 to deliver Israel from the Midianites with three hundred men; his victory with torches and jars illustrates that God's strength is made perfect in weakness
